examples.english: The allied forces bombarded the enemy trenches for weeks.
examples.chinese: 盟军炮轰敌军战壕，历时数周。

examples.english: Cromwell's men had been bombarding the fort with their artillery for several days.
examples.chinese: 连日来，克伦威尔的部下一直用大炮轰击堡垒。

examples.english: I was afraid they were going to shoot us.
examples.chinese: 我担心他们会开枪打我们。

examples.english: Rico had been shot by a member of a rival gang.
examples.chinese: 里科被敌对黑帮的一名成员开枪打中了。

expressions.text: shoot somebody in the back/chest/leg etc

examples.english: He had been shot in the chest but managed to crawl to safety.
examples.chinese: 他胸部中弹，但还是设法爬到了安全的地方。

examples.english: A tourist was shot dead by muggers in New Orleans last night.
examples.chinese: 昨晚在新奥尔良，一名游客被行凶抢劫者开枪打死。

examples.english: Local militiamen shot down a federal army helicopter as it flew over the capital.
examples.chinese: 一架联邦军用直升机飞过州府上空的时候，当地民兵将它击落。

examples.english: They said the plane had been on a spy mission and they were justified in shooting it down.
examples.chinese: 他们说这架飞机是在执行间谍任务，被击落是罪有应得。

examples.english: I didn't realize he'd been hit until he fell to the ground.
examples.chinese: 等到他倒在地上，我才知道他被击中了。

examples.english: One of our planes has been hit.
examples.chinese: 我们的一架飞机被击中了。

expressions.text: be hit in the chest/face etc

examples.english: He was hit in the arm by a sniper's bullet but carried on fighting.
examples.chinese: 他的手臂被狙击手的子弹击中，但他还是继续战斗。

examples.english: The bank robbers gunned down two employees who tried to stop them getting away.
examples.chinese: 抢劫银行的歹徒开枪击倒了两名试图阻止他们逃跑的职员。

examples.english: Two men dragged him out of his home, and gunned him down in the street.
examples.chinese: 两名男子把他从家中拖出来，开枪把他击倒在街上。

examples.english: Jesse hid behind a rock and picked off the sheriff's men one by one as they rode past.
examples.chinese: 杰西躲在岩石背后，等警长部下骑马经过时就开枪逐个把他们打死。

examples.english: Our rifles were much more powerful and we were able to pick the enemy off before they could even fire at us.
examples.chinese: 我们的来复枪火力大多了，敌人还没来得及朝我们开枪，我们就把他们逐个击毙了。

centers.english: to be shot at by someone

examples.english: I heard a bullet whistle past my ear, and I realized we were being shot at.
examples.chinese: 我听到一颗子弹在我耳边呼啸而过，就意识到有人在朝我们开枪。

examples.english: The UN troops shouldn't be there just to be shot at -- they should be allowed to defend themselves.
examples.chinese: 联合国部队不该就在那里任人射击一应该允许他们自卫。

examples.english: The men's faces were white with fear -- none of them had ever been under fire before.
examples.chinese: 那些人吓得面如土色——他们以前从没遭到过射击。

examples.english: Although they were under heavy fire from all sides, they managed to get the wounded off the battlefield.
examples.chinese: 虽然四面八方都有炮火猛烈的射击，他们还是设法把伤员运出了战场。

examples.english: Troops sent to quell the fighting came under fire themselves.
examples.chinese: 被派去平息战乱的军队自己也遭到了射击。

examples.english: Two civilians were killed when they were caught in the crossfire between the police and the protestors.
examples.chinese: 两个平民在警方和抗议者的交叉火力中不幸身亡。

centers.english: to point a gun or weapon carefully before shooting
centers.chinese: 射击之前把枪或武器瞄准

examples.english: He picked up his shotgun, aimed, then fired.
examples.chinese: 他拿起猎枪，瞄准，然后开枪。

examples.english: The firing squad were already aiming their rifles and waiting for the order to shoot.
examples.chinese: 行刑队已经举枪瞄准，听候命令准备射击了。

examples.english: Which part of the target were you aiming at?
examples.chinese: 你瞄准的是目标的哪个部分？

examples.english: You can tell he was a professional killer -- they always aim for the chest.
examples.chinese: 看得出他是个职业杀手—他们总是瞄准胸膛。

examples.english: The rocket-launchers are aimed at Washington.
examples.chinese: 火箭发射筒瞄准了华盛顿。

examples.english: For those few seconds when they are taking aim, the soldiers are exposed to enemy fire.
examples.chinese: 士兵们在瞄准目标的那几秒钟里暴露在敌人的炮火之下。

examples.english: His first shot missed. The second hit its target.
examples.chinese: 他第一枪打歪了，第二枪打中了目标。

examples.english: Police fired shots into the air and used water cannon to disperse the crowd.
examples.chinese: 警察朝天鸣枪，并用高压水炮驱散人群。

examples.english: Oswald was seen running away from the building just after the shooting.
examples.chinese: 有人看到奥斯瓦尔德就在枪击案发生后逃离了那幢建筑物。

examples.english: There has been an alarming increase in the number of shootings on our streets.
examples.chinese: 我们街头的枪击事件在以惊人的速度增加。

examples.english: At least 4 people were killed by gunfire when police stormed the building.
examples.chinese: 警方突袭那幢大楼的时候，至少有四人死于炮火之中。

examples.english: Joseph sprinted away to dodge the volley of gunfire.
examples.chinese: 约瑟夫飞奔着躲避那枪林弹雨。

examples.english: A soldier was killed during an exchange of gunfire at the border station.
examples.chinese: 一名士兵在边防站的一次交火中丧生。

examples.english: The ship was hit by fire from a German plane.
examples.chinese: 轮船被德军飞机上射来的炮火击中。

examples.english: There was a sudden burst of machine gun fire.
examples.chinese: 突然，机关枪一阵猛烈扫射。

examples.english: We noticed that the enemy fire was now being directed at our part of the field.
examples.chinese: 我们注意到，敌人的火力现在转向了我们这一边。

examples.english: Before it was lowered into the ground, a volley of shots was fired over the General's coffin.
examples.chinese: 将军的灵柩入土前，其上面排枪齐射。

examples.english: The soldiers fired a volley into the air as a warning to the crowd.
examples.chinese: 士兵们向空中放排枪警告人群。

examples.english: The Germans began their bombardment of Paris in early 1870.
examples.chinese: 1870年初，德军开始炮轰巴黎。

examples.english: The devastating air bombardment of the last four weeks is only the latest of a series of assaults by foreign armies.
examples.chinese: 过去四个星期里的毁灭性空中轰炸只是外国军队最新的一次行动，他们已经发动了一系列的袭击。

examples.english: US warplanes continued their barrage again this morning.
examples.chinese: 今天早晨美国战机又继续密集轰炸。

examples.english: a barrage of machine-gun fire
examples.chinese: 密集的机关枪火力

examples.english: Soon after dawn there was another round of heavy shelling in the eastern part of the city.
examples.chinese: 天亮不久，城东遭遇另一轮密集的炮火攻击。

examples.english: Wallace died in a hail of bullets in Los Angeles, the victim of a drive-by killing.
examples.chinese: 华莱士在洛杉矶遇上了飞车枪击事件，在枪林弹雨中遇害身亡。

examples.english: One witness claimed she had heard eight shots.
examples.chinese: 一位目击者称她听到了八声枪响。

examples.english: An occasional gunshot can still be heard, but no-one knows who fires them.
examples.chinese: 仍能听到零星的枪声，但是没有人知道是谁开枪。

examples.english: Shots rang out from across the street as someone tried to break up the fight.
examples.chinese: 有人试图劝架，这时街对面传来了几声枪响。

examples.english: Enemy gunfire could be heard from several kilometres away.
examples.chinese: 敌军的炮火几公里以外都听得到。

examples.english: The earth shook with the sound of heavy gunfire.
examples.chinese: 大地也被猛烈的炮火声震动了。

examples.english: Two gunmen opened fire on a bus taking children to school.
examples.chinese: 两名持枪歹徒朝一辆接孩子上学的公共汽车开了枪。

examples.english: Was President Kennedy killed by a lone gunman, or was there a conspiracy?
examples.chinese: 刺杀肯尼迪总统是独行枪手所为，还是一项阴谋？

examples.english: Hooded gunmen burst into a home in Lima on Sunday and shot to death at least 15 people.
examples.chinese: 星期天一群戴着风帽的持枪歹徒闯入利马的一所住宅，开枪打死了至少15人。

examples.english: Weapons were found at three locations believed to be used by snipers.
examples.chinese: 在三处地方找到相信是狙击手使用的武器。

examples.english: A sniper's bullet pierced his windshield and hit him in the eye.
examples.chinese: 一名狙击手的子弹穿过挡风玻璃，击中了他的眼睛。

examples.english: Police marksmen surrounded the building.
examples.chinese: 警方的神枪手包围了大楼。

examples.english: A marksman was called in to try and hit the enemy's machine gun post.
examples.chinese: 一名神枪手奉召到场，试图打掉敌人的机枪点。

examples.english: You'd have to be a really good shot to get that bird from here.
examples.chinese: 你得有非常好的枪法才能从这儿把那只鸟打下来。

examples.english: I used to be the best shot in the whole school.
examples.chinese: 我以前是全校枪法最好的人。
